mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue visibility and drives sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in forward 60-day returns during periods where INDPRO and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, further rate hikes signal aggressive liquidity withdrawal that compresses equity risk premiums and forces negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25 percent, or the negative predictive relationship reverses to positive during historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate profit growth, producing a continuous positive relationship between GDP expansion and short-term S&P 500 returns regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.10 for three consecutive years, or GDP contractions do not precede negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 600,000 demonstrates robust employment creation that sustains consumer demand, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of periods where PAYEMS accumulates over 600,000, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicates supply-constrained demand that erodes real margins and forces negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously, or the interaction effect vanishes during historical periods of supply-side inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit expansion phase, rising residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects increased household leverage and spending power, which directly fuels positive short-term equity returns for consumer-facing sectors.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative when RSAFS increases during credit expansion periods, or the relationship reverses when household debt-to-income ratios exceed historical 90th percentiles.
